OBJECTION TO AND MOTION TO STRIKE NEW MATTER SUBMITTED WITH PLTFS' REPLY PAPERS AND, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE AND OPPOS FILED BY DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. DAVOREN, STEPHEN V.

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS. GRANTED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS IT RELATES TO THE FINANCIAL INFORMATION OF DEFENDANTS EXCEPT AS TO THOSE ITEMS DEFENDANT CLAIM ARE PRIVILEGED OR CONFIDENTIAL. AS TO THOSE ITEMS, DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG PURSUANT TO CCP 2031.240. PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER REGARDING DISAGREEMENTS IN PRODUCTION AND ALSO DISCUSS A PROTECTIVE ORDER. PRODUCTION AND PRIVILEGE LOG ARE DUE 20 DAYS FROM SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. MOTION DENIED AS IT RELATES TO TAX RETURNS. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED, BOTH PARTIES ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. JUDGE CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, NOT REPORTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER)

MOTION And Notice Of Motion Pursuant To Ccp Sect 724.050 Et Seq. For Order Requiring Judgment Creditor To Sign Satisfaction Of Judgment And To Reimburse Moving Party'S Reasonable Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: HOLDEN STEIN, ESQ., STEIN LAW GROUP, 415.321.7190, FOR PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY.
MARK MOSLEY, ESQ., SEILER EPSTEIN ZIEGLER & APPLEGATE, LLP, 415.979.0500, FoR DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.'S Motion Pursuant To CCP Section 724.050 Et. Seq. For Order Requiring Judgment Creditor To Sign Satisfaction Of Judgment And To Reimburse Moving Party's Reasonable Attorney's Fees is denied without prejudice to being re-filed. The judgment is not satisfied because defendant still owes plaintiff post-judgment fees. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Ntc Of Motion Of Pltf For Post Judgment Costs Pursuant To Ccp 685.080
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: HOLDEN STEIN, ESQ., STEIN LAW GROUP, 415.321.7190, FOR PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY.
MARK MOSLEY, ESQ., SEILER EPSTEIN ZIEGLER & APPLEGATE, LLP, 415.979.0500, FPR DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY'S Motion For Post Judgment Costs Pursuant To CCP 685.080 is granted in part. Plaintiff is awarded an additional $10,000 as reasonable fees and costs. While plaintiff is entitled to an award of additional fees and costs, the amount sought is hugely excessive given the amount of the underlying judgment, the post-judgment work for which additional fees and costs are sought, and defendant's belated effort to satisfy the judgment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Notice And Motion To Compel Deft John B. Logvy Manufacturing Ocmpany, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Specially Prepared Interrogatories(Set One) And Request For Productin Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CLEANWELL COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Defendant John B. Logvy Manufacturing Company, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Granted in part. Motion granted as it relates to the financial information of defendants except as to those items defendant claim are privileged or confidential. As to those items, defendants shall prepare and provide a privilege log pursuant to CCP 2031.240. Parties must meet and confer regarding disagreements in production and also discuss a protective order. Production and privilege log are due 20 days from service of this order. Motion denied as it relates to tax returns. Request for sanctions is denied, both parties acted with substantial justification. Judge Charlotte Walter Woolard, not reported. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order) =(302/CWW)
